message:
fix(reactor): preserve bookkeeping on hard epoll/kqueue delete failure

EpollReactor::deregister() previously always removed token/fd
bookkeeping even when the kernel epoll_ctl(DEL) call failed with an
unexpected error (e.g., EBADF on the epoll fd itself, not the target).
This meant a retry after the transient failure would find no
bookkeeping and silently succeed without actually deregistering.

Now bookkeeping is only cleaned up on success or benign errors (ENOENT,
EBADF when the target fd is already closed). Hard failures preserve
bookkeeping so the caller can retry deregister() after resolving the
underlying issue.

Kqueue reactor receives the same treatment.

New test: deregister_hard_delete_failure_preserves_bookkeeping_for_retry
simulates the scenario by temporarily closing the poller fd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
